title: 🌮 Ordering tacos in Houston
intro: A busy taquería in Houston — Spanish only. Walk up and order. The taquero's lines change each time, so you have to actually listen and respond.

beats.say_en: Hey! What can I get you?
beats.hint: Greet and start your order — say how many tacos and, if you want, what kind.
beats.model: Hola, quiero tres tacos, por favor.
beats.model_en: Hi, I want three tacos, please.

beats.say_en: What kind (of meat) do you want them?
beats.hint: Pick a meat: pastor, asada, pollo, carnitas, suadero, barbacoa…

beats.say_en: With everything? Cilantro and onion? / Spicy or not?
beats.hint: Say yes to everything, or ask for no onion / mild salsa.
beats.model: Con todo, pero poca salsa, por favor.
beats.model_en: With everything, but not much salsa, please.

beats.hint: Order a drink (agua, refresco, horchata) or say that's all.
beats.model: Una horchata, y eso es todo.

beats.hint: Answer: para aquí (here) or para llevar (to go).

say: Le doy cuatro con cincuenta, joven.
beats.hint: Ask if they take card, or say here's the cash.
beats.model_en: Do you take card? … Here you go, thanks.

beats.say_en: Thanks, take care! / Enjoy, come back soon!

outro: ¡Listo! You ordered, customized, and paid — entirely in Spanish. That's a real transaction handled. 🌮
